case_name: Galich v. Advocate Health and Hospital Corp.

summary.brief: Galich v. Advocate Health and Hospital Corp.. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Steven Butts went to an emergency room with a broken jaw. The emergency room doctor allegedly failed to properly intubate Butts, depriving him of oxygen and causing permanent brain damage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After a 10-day trial, a jury returned a verdict for Butts, awarding $45.3 million in damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Galich, as Butts’s guardian, filed a complaint against Dr. Joo, alleging negligence, as well as vicarious liability claims against Advocate.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Testifying for Galich, Dr. John Downs, an anesthesiologist and critical care physician, opined that Dr. Joo should have examined Butts’s airway before administering the paralytic and sedative medications.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. John Downs

evidence_index.text: Dr. Downs opined Dr. Joo’s negligence in depriving Butts of sufficient oxygen was the sole cause of his brain damage.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. John Downs

evidence_index.text: Dr. Baugh also opined that Dr. Joo violated the standard of care by giving Butts insufficient oxygen while he was sedated, which, after a prolonged period, caused permanent brain damage.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Christopher Baugh

evidence_index.text: Dr. Edward Ward, an emergency medicine doctor, opined it was appropriate and within the standard of care for Dr. Joo to try to intubate Butts without first calling for assistance.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Edward Ward

evidence_index.text: Butts suffered oxygen deprivation and catastrophic brain injury as a result of Dr. Joo’s negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury’s verdict indicated they unanimously agreed Dr. Joo’s negligence caused Butts’s brain injury. That is all the law requires. We affirm the judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: Butts went into pulseless electrical activity and cardiac arrest at about 5:45 a.m., necessitating a “code blue.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Galich, who was in the exam room, agreed that Dr. Joo tried twice to intubate her son before asking someone to call anesthesia for assistance.
evidence_index.speaker: Gail Galich

evidence_index.text: Butts’s heart rate, respiration rate, and blood oxygen levels were within normal ranges, and he was alert and well-oriented.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Joo advised Butts he wanted to intubate him to prevent his airway from destabilizing during the ambulance transfer. Butts consented.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Asokumar Buvanendran, an anesthesiology expert, opined that Dr. Joo complied with the standard of care by calling anesthesiology for assistance after unsuccessfully attempting to intubate Butts.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Asokumar Buvanendran

evidence_index.text: The trial court concluded that “[t]he jury need not unanimously agree as to which of several alternative acts of the defendant constituted the negligence, as long as the jury unanimously agrees that the evidence supports the conclusion that the defendant was negligent.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: History of military deployment in Iraq and Afghanistan

initial_state.chief_complaint: Facial injuries after falling from a second-floor balcony
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with bilateral jaw fractures and headache, but was walking, talking, and breathing without difficulty.

focused_exam.head_and_neck: Missing tooth, lacerated chin, bleeding from the face, unable to fully open mouth due to pain from broken jaw

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Facial trauma with potential airway compromise
initial_differential: Airway compromise due to facial trauma

reveal.result_summary: Anesthesiology was paged for assistance after failed intubation attempts
reveal.decision_impact: Should have been consulted prior to intubation attempts

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with facial trauma

key_events.event: Initial assessment and decision to intubate

timeline_phases.name: Airway Management
timeline_phases.description: Attempts to secure airway and subsequent complications

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with facial trauma and potential airway compromise
decision_points.prompt: What should be the approach to secure the airway?

options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures expert assistance in difficult airway management
Risky due to potential complications in airway management

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant attempted intubation without anesthesiology consultation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it was appropriate to proceed without consultation

expected_action_court.description: Anesthesiology consultation should have been sought before intubation
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for consultation in complex airway cases

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to consult anesthesiology led to improper airway management
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Anesthesiology should have been involved from the start
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered oxygen deprivation and catastrophic brain injury

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to provide sufficient oxygen
Causation established - negligence led to brain injury

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Adequate oxygenation would have prevented brain injury

diagnoses.diagnosis: Permanent brain damage due to oxygen deprivation

complications.complication: Oxygen deprivation leading to brain injury

court_findings.finding: Dr. Joo breached duty of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Breach led to oxygen deprivation and brain injury

court_findings.finding: Dr. Joo breached duty of care
